asha.name: Asha
asha.want: To keep her junction of the network alive. That's all she has left.
asha.fear: That she's only burning out of habit, not purpose. That her partner would be ashamed of how small her fire has become.
asha.contradiction: She tells everyone to hurry, to save the network — but she herself hasn't moved from this spot in 200 years. She's the one who needs saving.
asha.backstory: Was a Root Keeper before the network started dying. Her partner's word was Light — the Storm ate them in the first wave.
asha.why_helps: You remind her of her partner. Not because of your word, but because you showed up. Her partner always showed up.

ren.name: Ren
ren.want: To feel something real one more time before they disappear completely.
ren.fear: That they've already disappeared and just haven't noticed yet.
ren.contradiction: They warn you not to echo, not to sacrifice yourself — but that's exactly what they did, over and over, for others. They don't regret it. They'd do it again.
ren.backstory: Was once a powerful Hollow — could absorb anything. Echoed too many times trying to save people. Now barely a person.
ren.why_helps: You didn't look away when you saw them flickering. Everyone else does.

luma.name: Luma
luma.want: To make something grow. Anything. One thing.
luma.fear: That being Seed means being potential forever and never becoming anything real.
luma.contradiction: They ARE growing things — their tears are what's keeping the dead words dormant instead of fully dead. They just can't see it because growth doesn't always look like growth.
luma.backstory: A child who waters dead words in the root garden. Their word is Seed but nothing they plant grows. They've been crying into the soil because they think they're broken.
luma.why_helps: They're a mirror of the villain. Same word (Seed), opposite response to the same pain. Luma waters. Kael eats.

kael.name: Kael
kael.want: Equality. If everyone has the same word, nobody is trapped by their birth-word.
kael.fear: That he was right to be Seed all along. That what he called a prison was actually a gift he destroyed.
kael.contradiction: He wants freedom for everyone but achieved his own freedom through violence. He wants no one trapped but he's the one doing the trapping.
kael.backstory: The only person who ever changed their own word. Was born Seed — potential forever, never becoming. Hated it so much he tore his own word out and replaced it with Storm. Each word he consumed was someone who asked him to. Dying souls who said 'take mine, at least it won't go to waste.' He started as mercy and became addiction.
kael.why_helps: He doesn't help. But he understands. 'Do you know what it's like to be potential forever?'

the_elder.name: The Elder
the_elder.want: To prepare the next generation without causing panic.
the_elder.fear: That preparation is impossible — that knowing what's coming would break people faster than the thing itself.
the_elder.contradiction: Keeps secrets to protect others, but secrecy is what leaves them unprepared.
the_elder.backstory: Connects the village to the root network. Knows more than they say. Their reason for secrecy: they know what's coming and believe preparation would cause panic worse than the threat.
the_elder.why_helps: You are the village's hope, whether you know it or not.

seri.name: Seri
seri.want: To see beyond the village. To know what the root network looks like from the inside.
seri.fear: Being ordinary. Getting a boring word and living a boring life in the village forever.
seri.contradiction: She craves adventure but freezes in real danger. Her word is Drift — she can move through anything — but she drifts away from the things that matter too.
seri.backstory: Grew up next door to the player. Always the brave one, always the first to climb higher, run further. Gets her word before the player does. Her emergence changes things between you.
seri.why_helps: She's your oldest friend. That doesn't go away, even when everything else does.

mother.name: Mother
mother.want: To keep you safe. To give you what she didn't have — a choice about whether to go below.
mother.fear: That you'll go into the network and not come back. Like her sister did.
mother.contradiction: She tells you the network is dangerous, to stay in the village — but she named you after a word. She wants you to be extraordinary and safe, and those two things don't coexist.
mother.backstory: Her sister went into the root network twenty years ago and never returned. She married a quiet man and tried to build a life that didn't need the network. Then you were born, and she saw the same look in your eyes.
mother.why_helps: She gives you a keepsake before you descend. What it is depends on her word. It's always the thing she most wishes she could keep.
